Jai Bhavani, Jai Shivaji

# Jai Bhavani, Jai Shivaji

### My tribute to Chattrapati Shivaji Maharaj

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Feb 19, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

Share

Chattrapati Shivaji Maharaj, a legend, a warrior, a wise ruler, a leader. It is not possible to fit the life of such a great man in one single post. So on his Jayanti today, this is a compilation of my posts here on him.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!MW4X!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1b3cde02-28a1-415f-8174-5423e8357557_679x459.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!MW4X!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1b3cde02-28a1-415f-8174-5423e8357557_679x459.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

*Pratapgarh, the fort that started Shivaji Maharaj’s victory quest, located in Satara district, aroud 23 km west of Mahabaleshwar. Standing 1080 metres above sea level, on a narrow spur between the villages of Par and Kinesvar, the fort was constructed by Shivaji’s Prime Minister Moropant Pingle. The upper half of the fort, is roughly square, 180 m long on either side, and has a temple dedicated to Mahadev. The lower fort is around 320 m long, 110m wide, at southeast of the fort, defended by towers and bastions.*

[Battle of Pratapgarh](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2019/08/29/battle-of-pratapgarh/)

*The victory at Battle of Kolhapur and the conquest of Vishalgarh, gave Shivaji a decisive edge, as he began to occupy more and more of Adilshahi territory. It also led to Aurangzeb taking notice of him, and the rising Maratha power.*

[Battle of Kolhapur](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2019/09/12/battle-of-kolhapur/)

*This pass would however be remembered for a historic battle fought here on February 2, 1661, when Chattrapati Shivaji defeated Kartalab Khan, the right hand man of Shaishta Khan, one of the most decisive victories ever, that further consolidated his position and strengthened the Marathas.*

[Battle of Umberkhind](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2020/01/31/battle-of-umberkhind/)

*Baji Prabhu Deshpande’s sacrifice was on par with Tanaji Malusare’s at Singhad, Shivaji Maharaj renamed Ghor Khind to Paavan Khind( Sacred Pass) in honor of the great man’s sacrifice and bravery. And honored his family with maanache pahile paan( Court Honor).*

[Battle of Pavan Khind](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2020/07/12/battle-of-pavan-khind/)

*When Shivaji, came to the fort, he refused to celebrate the capture, seeing his closest friend, and ablest general, lying dead. He broke down saying**“Gad Aala, Pan Sinha Gela” , meaning “I have won the Fort, but lost the lion”.***

[Battle of Sinhagad](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2007/04/10/sinhgad/)

*While a lot is known about Shivaji Maharaj’s military conquests and battles, I feel not much focus has been laid on his administration and the way he built up the military. Shivaji has rightly been regarded as one of the finest warriors, a great general, a brilliant strategist and a renowned exponent of guerrilla warfare. His role in raising a dynasty and and independent kingdom, during the reign of the Mughals, and the way he gave nightmares to Aurangzeb has been the stuff of heroism and legend.*

[Shivaji- The Ruler](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2015/02/19/shivaji-the-ruler/)

*Quite often, growing up in the shadow of a banyan tree can be quite inhibiting, however much you grow, and you shall always look small against the banyan. In a sense that was what happened to Sambhaji, the son of Sivaji. Growing up in the shadow of a powerful father, who was a giant of a personality, meant Sambhaji, for all his achievements would never really match up to Sivaji.*

[Sambhaji](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2015/05/14/chattrapati-sambhaji/)

Tarabai Bhonsle, the daughter-in-law of Chattrapathi Shivaji Maharaj, wife of Rajaram, who had entered the royal household when she was just 8 years old. Her father Hambirrao Mohite, was the chief military commander of Shivaji’s army, who was one of his most loyal associates and had taken part in many a campaign. And also niece to Soyrabai, one of Shivaji’s wives and mother of Rajaram. When her husband passed away, she placed her four year old son on throne and took charge as regent.

[Rani Tarabai](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2020/12/09/rani-tarabai/)
